title: workbook.xlsx.writeFile writes Zero byte files
author: raj7desai
body:
Trying to write some XLSX files but when i run it, it creates files which are Zero bytes and cannot be opened by excel. 

Similar to : https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40519084/node-exceljs-is-writing-0-byte-file

comments.author: guyonroche
comments.body: @raj7desai could you post code that demonstrates this?

comments.author: ghost
comments.body:
Actually I am facing with the same problem. I can get any excel file without writing any new. But when I try to read an excel file and write it into a new file and send that file I get a 0kb excel file that won't open.

`var workbook = new Excel.Workbook(); `
`workbook.xlsx.readFile('old.xlsx').then(function() {`
`    workbook.xlsx.writeFile('new.xlsx');`
`    res.download('new.xlsx');`
`}).catch(err => {`
`    res.send("Error: " + err);`
` });`

By the way, I am using Google Cloud Functions if it matters.

comments.author: omarsafwany
ghost
comments.body: As far as I understand this problem occurs when the directory is read-only. You can put files before deployment but after deployment, you can only read them.

comments.author: omarsafwany
comments.body: I am trying to write a file from scratch but I the file is zero bytes. When I try to open it, I get an error stating `file format is invalid`

comments.author: guyonroche
comments.body:
@emrektlc I can see a problem in the code you posted...
both workbook.xlsx.readFile() and workbook.xlsx.writeFile() are both asynchronous and return promises. You're waiting for the read to complete but not the write

comments.author: omarsafwany
raj7desai
Xavier000
comments.body:
I'm facing the same issue @raj7desai 
Any update?

comments.author: LouisVA
comments.body: @raj7desai Have you tried using the stream ? I had issues with another excel library when using a writeFile as the files got larger. Using Excel.stream.xlsx.WorkbookWriter() solved that for me.
